product_overview: Frontend SPA for a real-time building energy optimization platform that visualizes a digital twin, historical reports, and predictive maintenance data. It receives live BuildingSnapshot data via WebSocket and uses REST for initial loads and historical queries.
core_goals: Provide a real-time digital twin visualization of zone temperatures and equipment status using WebSocket streaming.
Offer historical performance and savings analysis via REST polling for reporting and benchmarking.
Surface predictive maintenance insights and detailed asset metrics with clear health/status indicators.
Keep UI stateless (dumb components) with hooks and stores owning data logic, and use environment-driven configuration.
Maintain a resilient WebSocket connection with auto-reconnect and clear connection-state surfaced in the topbar.

features.name: App Shell & Navigation
features.description: Global application shell providing sidebar navigation (Digital Twin, Building Report, Predictive Maintenance), topbar live status pill, and AI chat bubble; mounts all views to preserve state between switches.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/building/snapshot -> Receive 200 with BuildingSnapshot (initial load) -> Open WebSocket ws://localhost:8000/ws -> WebSocket connection established and first BuildingSnapshot message received

GET /api/v1/building/snapshot -> Receive 500 Internal Server Error -> Open WebSocket ws://localhost:8000/ws -> WebSocket connection refused -> UI topbar shows DISCONNECTED and app falls back to periodic GET /api/v1/building/snapshot polling

features.name: WebSocket Live Connection
features.description: Persistent WebSocket connection to ws://localhost:8000/ws that streams BuildingSnapshot messages every simulated timestep with client-side auto-reconnect and connection-state exposed to the UI.
user_flows: Open WebSocket to ws://localhost:8000/ws -> WebSocket connection established (readyState=1) -> Receive repeated BuildingSnapshot messages (zone and equipment updates) -> Optionally send ACK over ws to confirm receipt

Open WebSocket to ws://localhost:8000/ws -> Initial connection attempt fails (network error) -> Client waits backoff interval -> Retry WebSocket connect to ws://localhost:8000/ws -> Connection established and streaming resumes

WebSocket to ws://localhost:8000/ws -> Connection established -> Receive BuildingSnapshot -> WebSocket unexpectedly closes -> Client initiates auto-reconnect attempt #1 to ws://localhost:8000/ws -> Reconnect succeeds and client receives resumed BuildingSnapshot stream

features.name: Digital Twin (Dashboard)
features.description: Default view showing a 3D building viewer (Three.js) with zone temperature color mapping, floor-plan SVG overlay, simulation delta bar, and AI recommendations panel driven by live BuildingSnapshot data.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/building/snapshot -> Receive 200 with BuildingSnapshot (zones, equipment, kpis) -> Open WebSocket ws://localhost:8000/ws -> Receive subsequent BuildingSnapshot ws messages with zone temperature deltas -> GET /api/v1/building/snapshot?mode=simulation&scenario=baseline -> Receive 200 with simulation snapshot for delta comparison

GET /api/v1/building/snapshot -> Receive 200 with BuildingSnapshot -> Open WebSocket ws://localhost:8000/ws -> Receive malformed BuildingSnapshot message (missing zones) -> Client logs parsing error and falls back to latest REST snapshot -> User triggers manual refresh: GET /api/v1/building/snapshot -> Receive corrected 200 BuildingSnapshot

features.name: Building Report
features.description: Historical performance and savings charts, thermal heatmap, and benchmark comparisons using REST polling and server-provided historical snapshot data.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/building/snapshot?range=24h -> Receive 200 with time-series BuildingSnapshot entries for last 24h -> GET /api/v1/building/snapshot?range=7d -> Receive 200 with aggregated 7d series -> Client renders Lightweight Charts for performance and savings

GET /api/v1/building/snapshot?range=7d -> Receive 200 with energy_kwh values all equal to 0 -> Client filters zero values and displays 'no historical savings data' guidance -> User retries with broader range: GET /api/v1/building/snapshot?range=30d -> Receive 200 with non-zero series

features.name: Predictive Maintenance
features.description: Equipment health and asset detail view with hierarchical TreeView, health gauges, timeline charts, and system diagram driven by live and historical BuildingSnapshot equipment metrics.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/building/snapshot -> Receive 200 with equipment list and metrics -> Open WebSocket ws://localhost:8000/ws -> Receive incremental equipment metric updates via BuildingSnapshot messages -> GET /api/v1/building/snapshot?equipment_id=ahu-1&range=24h -> Receive 200 with equipment time-series for timeline chart

GET /api/v1/building/snapshot?equipment_id=chiller-2 -> Receive 404 Not Found -> Open WebSocket ws://localhost:8000/ws -> Receive equipment metrics reporting chiller-2 status 'offline' -> UI shows asset as offline with remediation guidance

code_summary.description: Real-time building energy optimization platform frontend. Connects to a FastAPI backend via REST and WebSocket to visualize thermal data, equipment status, and simulation results for a digital twin.

features.name: Digital Twin (Dashboard)
features.description: 3D building viewer with Three.js, floor plan SVG overlay, simulation delta bar, and AI recommendations panel. Real-time zone temperatures and equipment status from WebSocket.

features.name: Building Report
features.description: Historical performance charts, savings analysis, thermal heatmap, and benchmark data. Uses REST polling for historical data.

features.name: Predictive Maintenance
features.description: Equipment health panel with tree view, health gauges, timeline chart, and HVAC system diagram. Shows chiller, AHUs, filters status.

app_shell.description: Main layout with sidebar navigation (3 views), topbar with live status indicator, WebSocket lifecycle management, and AI chat bubble overlay.

data_flow: WebSocket -> wsManager (lib/websocket.ts) -> Zustand store -> hooks -> components
REST API -> TanStack Query -> hooks -> components
key_types: ZoneData (id, name, temperature, setpoint, co2, occupancy)
